authoredShaderContract.materialGraph: chrome.002 Principled metal with Generated Noise → Map Range → Multiply → Roughness
authoredShaderContract.browserResolution: The existing Chrome Crayon material backend reconstructs the generated-coordinate noise branch and consumes the exact rough field. Since every authored rough value is zero, the mapped procedural result is multiplied to polished roughness zero in both renderers.

capture.lighting: Blender studio.exr and the byte-identical Three.js PMREM environment at intensity 0.8, plus the shared authored area-light rig

interpretation: Topology, silhouette, material choice, metallic base, and the zero-roughness field are verified. Fine reflection placement remains renderer-dependent because Eevee samples the source environment directly while Three.js uses a PMREM convolution.
